nodes.bodyText: The fetch request to the ComfyUI server lacks a timeout. If the server is unresponsive or the network is slow, this call could hang indefinitely. It is recommended to use an AbortController to implement a reasonable timeout for the request.

Watching systemPrompt and calling refreshActiveSystemMessage on every change can be expensive, especially since refreshActiveSystemMessage likely triggers storage persistence. If the user is editing the prompt in the UI, this will fire on every keystroke. Consider debouncing this watcher to improve performance.

Register a handler for headless artistry invoke
image_journal.create now calls artistryGenerateHeadless through Eventa, but this commit never registers a corresponding main-process invoke handler (there is no defineInvokeHandler/defineInvokeHandlers for that event in apps/stage-tamagotchi/src/main). In runtime this causes the invoke to fail immediately, so image generation from the tool path is broken even when provider settings are valid.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Pass Nano Banana globals into headless artistry config
The global config forwarded to generateHeadless omits nanobananaApiKey, nanobananaModel, and nanobananaResolution. NanoBananaProvider.initialize relies on those keys, so when the active provider is nanobanana the provider remains unconfigured and generate() throws Nano Banana API Key not configured despite the user filling the settings page.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Store card artistry settings under modules.artistry
Card saving writes artistry overrides to extensions.airi.artistry, but the rest of the runtime reads extensions.airi.modules.artistry (for example, card-to-environment sync and system prompt composition in useAiriCardStore). This mismatch means per-card artistry overrides and widget instruction can be silently ignored after save/switch.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Remove unresolved merge markers from source files
This commit includes raw conflict markers (<<<<<<<, =======, >>>>>>>), which makes the TypeScript/Vue sources unparsable and blocks the app from building or starting. I verified markers are present in apps/stage-tamagotchi/src/main/index.ts, packages/stage-pages/src/pages/settings/airi-card/components/CardCreationDialog.vue, and packages/stage-ui/src/stores/chat/session-store.ts, so this is a release-blocking merge artifact rather than a runtime edge case.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Read per-card artistry config from modules.artistry
image_journal.create reads card overrides from activeCard?.extensions?.airi?.artistry, but card normalization stores artistry under extensions.airi.modules.artistry (see resolveAiriExtension in packages/stage-ui/src/stores/modules/airi-card.ts). In practice this path is usually undefined, so per-card provider/model/prompt settings are ignored and generation silently falls back to global settings.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Strip bridge metadata from Replicate model input
request.extra is merged directly into inputOptions here, but artistry-bridge.ts injects internal fields like internalJobId and remixId into extra for tracking. When the active Replicate model uses a strict input schema, these unexpected keys are sent to replicate.run(...) and the generation request is rejected before inference starts. Filter out bridge-only keys before building the provider payload.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Permit Nano Banana generation without base image
This hard fail makes the Nano Banana provider unusable for normal image_journal.create calls that only provide a text prompt (no extra.image). In this commit, the create flow invokes headless generation with prompt/model/options but no source image, so selecting Nano Banana causes immediate errors ("requires a base image") instead of generating an image. Support text-only requests (or gate the provider in UI as image-edit-only) to avoid breaking the primary creation path.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Mark widget done only after provider completion
For providers that implement setJobCallback (currently all three new providers), this path skips polling and still sends status: 'done' immediately after generate(...) returns, even though generation continues asynchronously. That reports completion before an image or terminal error exists, so long-running jobs can appear falsely finished until a later callback (or indefinitely if callback delivery is missed). Emit the final done state only when a terminal callback/status is observed.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Import headless Eventa contract from a resolvable module
artistryGenerateHeadless is imported from ../../../shared/eventa, which resolves to src/main/shared/eventa from this file and does not exist (the event is defined in @proj-airi/stage-shared). This breaks module loading/registration in the main process, so the headless invoke path cannot be wired at runtime. Point this import to the actual shared contract source (or re-export it from the referenced module).
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Import new main-process services before invoking them
This callback now calls symbols like createContext, createServerChannelService, and defineInvokeHandler, but they are not imported in index.ts. In the main process this throws as soon as the callback runs (createContext is undefined), aborting startup wiring and leaving IPC/service initialization incomplete. Add explicit imports for the newly used helpers/events before using them here.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Restore lastEnterTime ref used by send-mode handlers
The local lastEnterTime state was removed, but handleMessageInputKeydown and the watch(sendMode, ...) block still read/write lastEnterTime.value. That causes a runtime ReferenceError when Enter handling or send-mode changes execute, breaking message sending in the chat input. Reintroduce the missing ref(0) declaration next to sendMode.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Reintroduce chat store imports in InteractiveArea
This component now references useChatOrchestratorStore, useChatSessionStore, and useChatStreamStore (and still renders ChatHistory) but those imports were removed, so these identifiers are unresolved in setup. In this codebase there is no auto-import plugin configured in apps/stage-tamagotchi/electron.vite.config.ts, so this breaks renderer compilation/runtime for the chat UI.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Define MCP config events before registering handlers
These symbols are imported and then passed to defineInvokeHandler, but apps/stage-tamagotchi/src/shared/eventa.ts does not export electronMcpGetConfig or electronMcpUpdateConfig. That leaves this module with missing bindings, so MCP service wiring cannot initialize correctly at startup.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Remove invalid stage-shared shortcuts re-export
This newly added re-export points to @proj-airi/stage-shared/shortcuts, but there is no shortcuts module in packages/stage-shared/src and no ./shortcuts subpath in packages/stage-shared/package.json exports. Importing this Eventa module will therefore try to resolve a non-existent module and fail build/startup.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Chain persistence tasks through a mutable queue
enqueuePersist no longer serializes writes because persistQueue is a constant and never updated; every call chains off the same already-resolved promise. That allows saveSession/saveIndex operations triggered by createSession, message updates, and imports to run concurrently and overwrite each other out of order, which can leave chat metadata and message history inconsistent on disk.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Preserve the session snapshot contract for chat sync
This refactor changes getSnapshot() to return only the index (and as a Promise), but the renderer sync layer still expects a synchronous payload containing activeSessionId, sessionMessages, and sessionMetas (apps/stage-tamagotchi/src/renderer/stores/chat-sync.ts). In follower/authority mode that contract mismatch breaks cross-window chat replication because snapshots no longer contain message state and may be posted as a non-cloneable Promise.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Define vision Eventa contracts before importing them
The new vision service imports visionCaptureScreen, visionCheckPermission, and visionRequestPermission from @proj-airi/stage-shared, but this commit's packages/stage-shared/src/index.ts does not export any of those symbols. Because this module is loaded from main startup wiring, unresolved named exports can fail bundling/module initialization and prevent the desktop app from starting.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Export sensor contracts from stage-shared before use
This service now imports sensor events from @proj-airi/stage-shared, but those exports are missing from packages/stage-shared/src/index.ts in the same commit. Since createSensorsService is called during main-process boot, unresolved imports block startup/handler registration and leave sensor IPC unavailable.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Pass quantization when loading Kokoro models
providers.ts now calls kokoroWorker.loadModel(device as any) in both the model-load and voice-list paths, but KokoroWorkerManager.loadModel still expects (quantization, device). This means the first argument becomes 'webgpu'/'cpu' as the quantization and the device is undefined, so Kokoro model initialization uses invalid parameters and local TTS model loading/voice refresh can fail or time out for configured users.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Keep Kokoro worker protocol compatible with manager
This worker now only handles 'load-model' and 'run-inference', but packages/stage-ui/src/workers/kokoro/index.ts still sends 'load'/'generate' and waits for 'loaded'/'result'. In practice, requests hit the worker's unknown-message path and never produce the expected response, so Kokoro local speech operations stall until timeout and trigger error/restart behavior instead of generating audio.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Remove undeclared refs from card initialization
initializeCard() now writes to identifiers like selectedActiveBackgroundId, generationEnabled, and acting prompt refs that are never declared/imported in this component, so opening the dialog will throw a ReferenceError before the form can render. Because initializeCard() is called during setup (const card = ref<Card>(initializeCard())), this breaks the card creation/edit flow at runtime, not just an optional code path.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Keep a valid route for kokoro-local provider settings
This commit deletes the dedicated Kokoro settings page, but provider cards still route speech providers to '/settings/providers/${provider.category}/${provider.id}'. Since kokoro-local is still exposed by the providers metadata, clicking it now navigates to /settings/providers/speech/kokoro-local with no matching page, so users cannot configure that provider from Settings.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Remove escaped quotes from ComfyUI template bindings
This SFC contains escaped quotes in template directives (for example v-model=\"comfyuiServerUrl\"), which makes Vue parse invalid binding expressions instead of normal directives. In practice the ComfyUI provider settings page cannot compile/render correctly, so users lose access to workflow upload and provider configuration from Settings.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Restore MCP SDK dependency in stage-tamagotchi package
The package manifest no longer declares @modelcontextprotocol/sdk, but main-process code still imports it in apps/stage-tamagotchi/src/main/services/airi/mcp-servers/index.ts. In isolated/frozen pnpm installs this leaves the module undeclared for this workspace and can break install/typecheck/bundling for MCP features.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Re-add kokoro-js dependency to stage-ui package
kokoro-js was removed from packages/stage-ui dependencies even though packages/stage-ui/src/workers/kokoro/worker.ts and .../types.ts still import it. That makes the workspace rely on accidental transitive resolution and can fail in clean pnpm installs or CI, blocking Kokoro local speech build/runtime paths.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Restore removed whisper composable export target
This commit deletes the whisper composable file, but the composables barrel still re-exports ./whisper (packages/stage-ui/src/composables/index.ts). Because many app surfaces import @proj-airi/stage-ui/composables, module resolution now fails during typecheck/bundling with a missing-file error before runtime.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Preserve per-card artistry options in normalized extensions
The normalized modules.artistry object copies only a subset of fields and drops options. Card editing and image_journal rely on modules.artistry.options for provider-specific overrides, so after loading/switching cards those overrides are silently discarded and generation falls back to global defaults.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Avoid gating artistry prompt on an unset enabled flag
The system prompt now includes artistry widget instructions only when modules.artistry.enabled is true, but this change set does not populate that flag in the card editing flow. For cards created/edited through the new UI, the condition stays false, so the configured artistry instruction is never injected into prompt composition.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Fall back to configured artistry provider for widget triggers
handleArtistryTrigger chooses providerId only from componentProps._artistryConfig and otherwise hard-defaults to comfyui, so widget updates that follow the default spawn/update flow (status + prompt) never use the user’s selected provider. In practice, users configured for Replicate/Nano Banana will still route to ComfyUI (and fail when no local ComfyUI is running), which breaks the primary artistry widget path outside image_journal.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Include widget payload overrides in provider request extras
The provider request is built from config.options only, so top-level widget payload fields (for example template and per-node overrides) are discarded before calling provider.generate. This conflicts with the new ComfyUI instruction flow (CardCreationTabArtistry.generateAgentInstructions) that tells the model to send those overrides in componentProps, causing workflow selection/parameter overrides to be silently ignored at runtime.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Point inference preload to an existing Kokoro adapter module
This import targets ../libs/inference/adapters/kokoro, but that module does not exist in the repo, so @proj-airi/stage-ui typecheck/bundling fails on module resolution before any preload logic can run. Because this file is part of the package sources, the broken import blocks builds even if useInferencePreload() is never called at runtime.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Initialize artistry providers for normal widget-trigger flow
Provider initialization is gated on config.Globals, but config comes from props._artistryConfig and that key is not populated by the standard widget update path (status: 'generating' + prompt). In that common path, providers run uninitialized: Replicate/Nano Banana will fail with missing API credentials and ComfyUI has no loaded workflow config, so artistry widget generations error out instead of starting.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Declare gpuu for the new stage-shared WebGPU import
This new file imports gpuu/webgpu, but packages/stage-shared/package.json does not declare gpuu. In strict/isolated pnpm installs, @proj-airi/stage-shared/webgpu can fail to resolve at build time because the dependency is undeclared for this workspace.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Nest artistry settings under modules when saving cards
The dialog currently saves artistry data at extensions.airi.artistry, but the runtime reads card-specific artistry config from extensions.airi.modules.artistry (for normalization, prompt composition, and tool overrides). In edit/create flows this means provider/model/prompt/options entered in this tab are not consumed as card module settings and generation falls back to global defaults. Please move this artistry block into the modules object when building cardWithModules.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Align global config schema with ComfyUI workflows payload
comfyuiSavedWorkflows is declared as a string, but the renderer sync path sends artistryGlobals with comfyuiSavedWorkflows as an array of workflow objects. That mismatch writes a config file that fails schema validation on next startup, causing createConfig(...).setup() to treat the config as invalid and drop persisted settings. The schema should accept the actual array shape (or the sync layer should serialize to the declared type).
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Keep beat-sync translation keys under modules.beat_sync
These entries were moved to settings.pages.modules.sections.*, but the beat-sync UI still reads settings.pages.modules.beat_sync.sections.* throughout packages/stage-pages/src/pages/settings/modules/beat-sync.vue. With this structure, section titles/descriptions and parameter labels on the Beat Sync settings page resolve to missing keys instead of localized text. Keep the section map nested under beat_sync.sections (or update every consumer path).
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Reset Replicate client when API key is cleared
This initializer only updates this.replicate when replicateApiKey is truthy, so clearing the API key in settings leaves the old authenticated client alive in memory and subsequent generations still run with stale credentials. In practice, users who remove/rotate keys can keep incurring requests with the previous token until restart; explicitly clear this.apiKey/this.replicate when the incoming key is empty.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Revoke Object URLs when deleting backgrounds
removeBackground drops the URL from backgroundUrls but never calls URL.revokeObjectURL for that entry. Because ensureObjectUrl creates blob URLs for every stored image, repeated add/remove cycles leak browser memory for the life of the renderer process, which can grow significantly with journal images.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Remove unresolved stage-shared webgpu export
This commit adds an exported subpath ./webgpu, but the referenced target src/webgpu/index.ts is not present in the commit tree. Any consumer importing @proj-airi/stage-shared/webgpu will fail module resolution at runtime/build time, so the export should be removed or backed by an actual module file.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Import ChatHistory in InteractiveArea
This component still renders <ChatHistory> in the template, but the script import was changed to only JournalPreviewModal. In this app there is no auto component-import plugin wired in apps/stage-tamagotchi/electron.vite.config.ts, so ChatHistory is unresolved at build/runtime and the chat UI cannot compile correctly. Fresh evidence: chat store imports were restored, but ChatHistory remains missing.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.
@dasilva333 check this comment

Allow repeated generation with identical prompts
Generation is gated by lastTriggerMap.get(id) !== triggerFingerprint, but this map entry is never cleared after success/failure. Once a widget has generated for a given prompt/remix fingerprint, sending status: "generating" with the same payload on that widget is permanently ignored, which breaks retry/regenerate flows for unchanged prompts.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Prefer explicit template overrides over card-level model
ComfyUIProvider.generate resolves templateId from request.model before request.extra.template, so widget payloads that explicitly pass a template cannot override the card default once a model is set. This conflicts with the new widget instruction flow (which tells the agent to send template in componentProps) and causes generations to run against the wrong workflow when users try per-request template switching.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Preserve legacy artistry fields when normalizing cards
The normalization now builds modules.artistry only from existingExtension.modules?.artistry and never falls back to legacy existingExtension.artistry, so older cards lose their saved provider/model/prompt/options at load time and silently revert to defaults until manually re-saved. This is a data-compatibility regression because the same commit still treats airi.artistry as a legacy source elsewhere.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Keep final widget update from clearing generated image
After a provider reports success, this code immediately sends a second update with only status/progress/actionLabel. In this codebase updateWidget replaces componentProps instead of merging them, so that second update drops the prior imageUrl/result fields and the artistry widget loses its generated image at completion. The same pattern appears in both completion paths, so normal widget-triggered generations can finish into a blank state.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Respect explicit seed value of 0 in ComfyUI overrides
The seed randomization check treats falsy values as unset (!overrides[nodeTitle]?.seed), so an explicit seed: 0 is overwritten with a random seed. For workflows where 0 is a valid deterministic seed, this breaks reproducibility and ignores a user-provided override.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Include source image in headless dedupe fingerprint
The headless dedupe key collapses every non-empty globals.image to 'HAS_IMAGE', so two in-flight generateHeadless requests with the same prompt/options but different source images will be treated as identical and share one pending promise. In image-edit/remix scenarios this can return the first request's output for the second request. Use an image-sensitive key (for example a hash) instead of presence-only when globals.image is part of generation input.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Verify cached background URL still exists before returning it
activeBackgroundUrl returns the cached backgroundUrls[lookupId] immediately, before confirming the entry still exists in entries. After a background is removed from another window, sync reload can drop the entry while leaving a stale URL in this map, causing deleted backgrounds to keep rendering (and old blob URLs to linger). Gate this return on entry existence or purge stale URL keys during reload.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Apply card artistry defaults to widget-triggered generations
Build widget requests from card/global artistry defaults when _artistryConfig is absent. In handleArtistryTrigger, prompt, model, and extra are derived only from config (props._artistryConfig), but the default widget flow in this commit sends only status and prompt, leaving config empty. In that common path, card-level settings saved under extensions.airi.modules.artistry (prompt prefix, model override, provider options) are never applied, so widget generations silently ignore per-card configuration.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Short-circuit disabled artistry provider before dispatch
The settings UI explicitly exposes a none provider as “Bypass and disable the image generation module globally”, but this bridge still tries to resolve it from artistryProviders and turns every generation request into a provider-not-found error. In practice, when users disable artistry and the assistant still emits an artistry/image_journal action, widget runs fail noisily instead of being treated as a disabled feature path.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Evict completed Replicate job results from in-memory cache
Completed jobs only clear callback handlers here, but jobResults entries are never removed after success/failure. Because every generation stores status (including URLs/error payloads), long-running sessions will accumulate an unbounded map and gradually increase memory usage. Add cleanup for jobResults alongside callback cleanup once a terminal state is reached.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Preserve failed status instead of forcing done
This completion branch marks a run as done even when the provider reports status: 'failed', which overwrites the error state and makes failed generations look successful. In practice, users can lose actionable failure messages (e.g., provider/network/model errors) and the widget ends in a misleading success terminal state despite no valid image output.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Include root componentProps overrides in artistry requests
The bridge only forwards props.payload into request.extra, so overrides sent at the root of componentProps (for example template or node field overrides) are dropped. This breaks the workflow contract documented in the new ComfyUI instructions (which tell agents to place overrides at the root), so custom template selection and exposed-field control are silently ignored.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Import watch before using the new tab-reset watcher
CardDetailDialog.vue now calls watch(() => props.modelValue, ...), but watch is not imported from vue in this file. Since this project’s Vite config does not provide Vue auto-imports, this leaves watch unresolved and breaks typecheck/bundling for the card settings dialog.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Pass widget id into Artistry close handler
handleClose only runs when props.id is present, but the widget host renders components from widget.componentProps and does not provide widget.id as a prop. In normal artistry flows, componentProps.id is absent, so clicking this close button becomes a no-op and the widget record is not removed.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Move ComfyUI health check off renderer CORS fetch
The connection test is executed in the renderer with fetch(..., { mode: 'cors' }), but actual ComfyUI generation requests run from the main process (apps/stage-tamagotchi/src/main/services/airi/widgets/providers/comfyui.ts) where CORS is not enforced. This creates false negatives for valid local ComfyUI setups (no CORS headers) and prompts users to weaken server settings unnecessarily. Route this check through a main-process invoke (or remove forced CORS mode) so the reported status matches real generation behavior.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Stop persisting card-scoped provider as global artistry default
This persists every synced payload.provider into artistryProvider in the global config file. The renderer emits that value from useArtistryStore().activeProvider (App.vue), and packages/stage-ui/src/stores/modules/airi-card.ts updates that store field whenever the active card changes, so switching cards rewrites the global default on disk. As a result, cards without explicit overrides can inherit the last viewed card’s provider after restart instead of the intended global setting.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Persist synced artistry provider before using config fallback
The bridge now falls back to appConfig.artistryProvider when _artistryConfig.provider is absent, but this sync handler only writes artistryGlobals and drops payload.provider. Fresh evidence: handleArtistryTrigger resolves providerId from config, yet artistrySyncConfig never updates that config field, so normal widget-triggered generations can keep routing to stale/default providers (commonly comfyui) after users switch providers.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Short-circuit disabled provider in headless generation
Handle the provider: 'none' case before looking up artistryProviders in the headless path. image_journal.create calls artistryGenerateHeadless, and when users select the documented "None (Disabled)" provider, this branch throws Provider 'none' not found instead of treating generation as intentionally disabled. That turns a configured-off feature into a runtime error for tool-driven image actions.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Should this go stage-ui? We will migrate to peserve many stage-ui configurations and keep them sync with Electron local config in another file.
globalApp config should only keep the necessary app config required for application to run correctly.
Could this be not as any?
Then you could have your own config for artistry without needed to mess with global app config.
This image is too big, could you please try turning it into avif by using pnpm to-avif <path to this image>
This image is too big, could you please try turning it into avif by using pnpm to-avif <path to this image>
This will affect stage-web, while stage-web have implemented some form of background already, would you mind using the same principle to avoid adding background directly to stage here.

Restore ModelCacheManager or remove its public export
Deleting this component leaves a dangling export in packages/stage-ui/src/components/scenarios/settings/index.ts (export { default as ModelCacheManager } ...), and that path is re-exported through components/index.ts. As a result, importing @proj-airi/stage-ui/components now resolves to a non-existent file and breaks typecheck/bundling even if the settings screen is never opened.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Keep inference module deletions consistent with live imports
This commit removes core inference files, but multiple consumers still import them (for example packages/stage-ui/src/libs/inference/adapters/kokoro.ts, .../adapters/whisper.ts, and packages/stage-ui/src/workers/background-removal/worker.ts still reference ../protocol, ../constants, and .../use-inference-status; devtools pages still import the deleted background-removal adapter path). That leaves hard module-resolution failures in stage-ui and downstream apps during typecheck/build.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Restore the removed stage-shared webgpu entrypoint
This commit removes the ./webgpu export from @proj-airi/stage-shared and deletes the backing module, but packages/stage-ui/src/stores/providers.ts still imports @proj-airi/stage-shared/webgpu (line 24 in this commit). That leaves stage-ui/apps with a hard module-resolution failure during typecheck/build whenever provider metadata is compiled, so the export/module must be restored or all consumers updated in the same change.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Keep Kokoro provider route backed by a settings page
Deleting this page breaks navigation for the still-registered kokoro-local speech provider: provider cards route to /settings/providers/${category}/${id} in the settings UI, and the provider catalog still includes kokoro-local, so selecting it now targets /settings/providers/speech/kokoro-local with no matching page component. Users can still see Kokoro in provider lists but can no longer open its configuration surface.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Read artistry config from store values instead of Ref wrappers
getArtistryConfig() force-casts useArtistryStore() to a ref-shaped snapshot, but this Pinia setup store is consumed elsewhere as unwrapped values (e.g. plain strings), so resolveArtistryConfigFromStore reads undefined from every .value access. In the image_journal.create flow this produces an effectively empty globals payload, and the main-process bridge then treats that empty object as the source of truth instead of persisted config, so headless generation can run without configured API keys/workflows and fail unexpectedly.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Reset active workflow when ComfyUI selection is cleared
The initializer only assigns activeWorkflowId when comfyuiActiveWorkflow is truthy, so syncing an intentional clear ('') leaves the previous workflow ID in memory. After users remove/switch workflows, generate() still resolves templates against that stale ID and can fail with a no-template error even though config was updated, until another non-empty workflow ID is set.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Bound widget polling loops with a timeout
Add a timeout/terminal escape to this polling loop, because it currently exits only on succeeded or failed. If a provider keeps returning running/queued (for example when a remote call hangs) or returns another terminal state like cancelled, this loop runs forever and keeps updating every 2s, leaving the widget stuck in generating state and leaking background work for the life of the process.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Include effective globals in headless dedupe fingerprint
This dedupe key is built before provider/default resolution and only includes params.provider/model/options plus image hash, so it ignores effective config that materially changes outputs (for example globals.comfyuiActiveWorkflow when model is unset). Two concurrent headless requests with the same prompt/options but different active workflow/provider defaults can collapse to one promise, causing the second caller to receive an image generated with the wrong workflow/config.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Read FileOutput URL string from Replicate correctly
This branch assumes FileOutput.url() returns a URL object and dereferences .href, but in Replicate's JS client it returns a string in common usage. When that happens, imageUrl becomes undefined and the code throws Output does not contain a recognizable image URL, so otherwise successful Replicate generations are marked as failed.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Accept data URLs as valid Replicate image outputs
The success check only accepts outputs whose URL starts with http, but Replicate can return data URLs for file outputs; in that case this path incorrectly throws and reports a failed generation even though an image payload exists. Treat data: URLs as valid output when setting succeeded status.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Reset artistry fields when card override is absent
Avoid falling back to the current store value here: when a user switches from a card that has artistry overrides to one that omits them, these ?? artistryStore.* assignments keep the previous card’s model/prompt/options instead of reverting to true defaults. That leaks generation settings across cards and can route the next card’s image requests with stale parameters.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Validate invalid artistry JSON before saving card
Do not silently coerce malformed JSON to undefined on save. In the current flow, a syntax error in the artistry options textarea still allows saving and overwrites previously valid modules.artistry.options with undefined, causing silent loss of provider overrides for that card.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Return fetchable base64 payloads from headless generation
downloadImageAsBase64 currently returns raw base64 bytes, but downstream renderer paths (image_journal.create and autonomous artistry) consume result.base64 via fetch(...), which requires a URL (typically a data: URL). With this return value, successful headless jobs can still fail at ingestion/save time because fetch cannot resolve the raw base64 string as an image resource. Prefix the payload as a data URL (or decode raw base64 at the call sites) so successful generations are actually usable.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Preserve card overrides when global artistry defaults change
These watchers always copy global values into the active runtime fields, which means editing global artistry settings can immediately overwrite the currently selected card’s provider/model/options. Card overrides are only re-applied in the watchDebounced(activeCard, ...) path when the active card changes, so generations can run with the wrong configuration until the user switches cards again.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Persist locale changes when app config is missing
This handler now updates the config only when config.get() returns an object. On first-run environments where configs:app has no file yet, get() is undefined, so changing language updates runtime locale but skips disk persistence; after restart the app falls back to default language again. The previous behavior wrote a new object even in this case, so this is a regression for fresh installs.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Guard autonomous artistry against concurrent runs
runArtistTask marks isProcessing true but never checks it before starting a new task, so multiple messages can launch overlapping analysis/generation jobs. In practice this can trigger duplicate billable image requests and nullify the intended 10s anti-rate-limit delay because several queued runs hit providers at once. Add a concurrency guard (or queue/cancel policy) at task entry.
